atoms.semantic_claim: The old better-payload emitter wrapper was updated with the richer empty-payload API, forwarding full BuildArguments rather than only PayloadConfig so metadata such as cancellation, cached state, and attempt identity can flow through wrapper layers.

destinations.evidence: Current wrapper still imports `HeaderForPayload` and `PayloadConfig`, and forwards only `config` to `self.inner.build_empty_payload(config)`.
Current trait still defines config-only empty payload construction, so full BuildArguments cannot be forwarded through this wrapper.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Downgraded after active-path review: the old full-BuildArguments empty-payload wrapper API is not exact-preserved, but active engine getPayload uses PayloadStore::resolve(), which now resolves with PayloadKind::WaitForPending. BasicPayloadJob filters out the empty-payload race for WaitForPending, so this wrapper empty-payload forwarding path is not part of the active payload FSM.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Add a wrapper-level regression where `BetterPayloadEmitter::build_empty_payload` receives non-default BuildArguments and assert the inner builder observes the same arguments rather than only a reconstructed config.
atoms.notes: Non-exact API/support difference only. Production engine getPayload goes through WaitForPending; explicit Earliest usage found only in e2e-test-utils.

summary: The BetterPayloadEmitter full-BuildArguments empty-payload API is not exact-preserved, but this is downgraded to a non-active API/support difference because active engine getPayload resolves with WaitForPending and does not race empty payloads.
notes: `BasicPayloadJob::resolve_kind` may construct an empty-payload receiver, but filters it out when kind is `WaitForPending`.
Explicit `PayloadKind::Earliest` usage found only in e2e-test-utils; no production engine caller found.
